reasoning: Given Hamas's core ideology of armed resistance and the explicit resolution criteria that rule out "plans contingent on future conditions," a formal policy announcement to disarm is highly improbable. Historically, any hints from Hamas about laying down arms have been fundamentally contingent on the establishment of an independent Palestinian state or a complete end to the occupation—conditions that would disqualify the announcement under these rules. Furthermore, militant organizations facing severe military degradation typically resort to underground insurgency rather than issuing formal, unconditional surrender or disarmament directives. Therefore, the chance of such an official, non-contingent announcement occurring before the end of 2025 is exceedingly low.
